segments.text: Every premium liquor brand uses heavy fancy glass bottles. [music] But this Indian gin comes in a steel zipper. But why would a luxury brand do this? Let me
show you how. It's a genius piece of packaging design. [music] Making a drink usually requires extra tools like a peg measure. Cherapundi Jin designed their
cap to double as a perfect measuring cup. You don't need something extra.
Packaging is the tool. But still, why use the metal body instead of glass?
Doesn't metal react with alcohol and ruin the taste? Not this one. They use a special high-grade stainless steel that keeps the gin pure. Plus, as a design
bonus, the metal chills your drink way faster than glass ever could. But there is a even bigger reason hidden behind this metallic bottle. Cherupunji is the
wetest place on earth. And this gin is actually made from purified rainwater.
The bottle was designed to look exactly like what vintage canteen locals use to collect [music] rain water in the mountains. It's not just packaging, it's
a piece of heritage. Subscribe for more amazing design content.
